answer_key.q9: human eye##human eye accommodation##Human Eye##Human Eye Accommodation

passages.title: Thomas Young
passages.source_html:
Thomas Young


A


Thomas Young (1773-1829) contributed 63 articles to the Encyclopedia Britannica, including 46 biographical entries (mostly on scientists and classicists) and substantial essays on ‘Bridge’, ‘Chromatics’, ‘Egypt’, ‘Languages’ and ‘Tides’. Was someone who could write authoritatively about so many subjects a polymath, a genius or a dilettante? In an ambitious new biography, Andrew Robinson argues that Young is a good contender for the epitaph ‘the last man who knew everything’. Young has competition, however: The phrase, which Robinson takes for his title, also serves as the subtitle of two other recent biographies: Leonard Warren’s 1998 life of paleontologist Joseph Leidy (1823-1891) and Paula Findlen’s 2004 book on Athanasius Kircher (1602-1680), another polymath.


B


Young, of course, did more than write encyclopedia entries. He presented his first paper to the Royal Society of London at the age of 20 and was elected a Fellow a week after his 21st birthday. In the paper, Young explained the process of accommodation in the human eye – on how the eye focuses properly on objects at varying distances. Young hypothesized that this was achieved by changes in the shape of the lens. Young also theorized that light traveled in waves and ho believed that, to account for the ability to see in color, there must be three receptors in the eye corresponding to the three ‘principal colors’ to which the retina could respond: red, green, violet. All these hypotheses were subsequently proved to be correct.


C


Later in his life, when he was in his forties, Young was instrumental in cracking the code that unlocked the unknown script on the Rosetta Stone, a tablet that was ‘found’ in Egypt by the Napoleonic army in 1799. The stone contains text in three alphabets: Greek, something unrecognizable and Egyptian hieroglyphs. The unrecognizable script is now known as demotic and, as Young deduced, is related directly to hieroglyphic. His initial work on this appeared in his Britannica entry on Egypt. In another entry, he coined the term Indo-European to describe the family of languages spoken throughout most of Europe and northern India. These are the landmark achievements of a man who was a child prodigy and who, unlike many remarkable children, did not disappear into oblivion as an adult.


D


Born in 1773 in Somerset in England, Young lived from an early age with his maternal grandfather, eventually leaving to attend boarding school. He had devoured books from the age of two, and through his own initiative, he excelled at Latin, Greek, mathematics and natural philosophy. After leaving school, he was greatly encouraged by his mother’s uncle, Richard Brocklesby, a physician and Fellow of the Royal Society. Following Brocklesby’s lead, Young decided to pursue a career in medicine. He studied in London, following the medical circuit, and then moved on to more formal education in Edinburgh, Gottingen and Cambridge. After completing his medical training at the University of Cambridge in 1808, Young set up practice as a physician in London. He soon became a Fellow of the Royal College of Physicians and a few years later was appointed physician at St. George’s Hospital.


E


Young’s skill as a physician, however, did not equal his skill as a scholar of natural philosophy or linguistics. Earlier, in 1801, he had been appointed to a professorship of natural philosophy at the Royal Institution, where he delivered as many as 60 lectures in a year. These were published in two volumes in 1807. In 1804 Young had become secretary to the Royal Society, a post he would hold until his death. His opinions were sought on civic and national matters, such as the introduction of gas lighting to London and methods of ship construction. From 1819 he was superintendent of the Nautical Almanac and secretary to the Board of Longitude. From 1824 to 1829 he was physician to and inspector of calculations for the Palladian Insurance Company. Between 1816 and 1825 he contributed his many and various entries to the Encyclopedia Britannica, and throughout his career, he authored numerous books, essays and papers.


F


Young is a perfect subject for a biography – perfect, but daunting. Few men contributed so much to so many technical fields. Robinson’s aim is to introduce non-scientists to Young’s work and life. He succeeds, providing clear expositions of the technical material (especially that on optics and Egyptian hieroglyphs). Some readers of this book will, like Robinson, find Young’s accomplishments impressive; others will see him as some historians have – as a dilettante. Yet despite the rich material presented in this book, readers will not end up knowing Young personally. We catch glimpses of a playful Young, doodling Greek and Latin phrases in his notes on medical lectures and translating the verses that a young lady had written on the walls of a summerhouse into Greek elegiacs. Young was introduced into elite society, attended the theatre and learned to dance and play the flute. In addition, he was an accomplished horseman. However, his personal life looks pale next to his vibrant career and studies.


G


Young married Eliza Maxwell in 1804, and according to Robinson, ‘their marriage was a happy one and she appreciated his work’. Almost all we know about her is that she sustained her husband through some rancorous disputes about optics and that she worried about money when his medical career was slow to take off. Very little evidence survives about the complexities of Young’s relationships with his mother and father. Robinson does not credit them, or anyone else, with shaping Young’s extraordinary mind. Despite the lack of details concerning Young’s relationships, however, anyone interested in what it means to be a genius should read this book.

groups.title: Questions 1-13
groups.source_html:
Questions 1-7


Do the following statements agree with the information given in the passage? Choose TRUE if the statement agrees with the information, choose FALSE if the statement contradicts the information, choose NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.

1

‘The last man who knew everything’ has also been claimed to other people.

正确答案： A. TRUE

原文答案句：Young has competition, however: The phrase, which Robinson takes for his title, also serves as the subtitle of two other recent biographies: Leonard Warren's 1998 life of paleontologist Joseph Leidy (1823-1891) and Paula Findlen's 2004 book on Athanasius Kircher (1602-1680), another polymath.


对应段落：A段


审题：题目问"The last man who knew everything"这个称号是否也被用于其他人。

定位关键信息：通过题干中的"The last man who knew everything"和"claimed to other people"这两个关键信息，可以在文章中定位到相关内容。


解题思路：在A段中，明确提到"The phrase, which Robinson takes for his title, also serves as the subtitle of two other recent biographies"，即这个短语也被用作其他两本传记的副标题，一本是关于Joseph Leidy的，另一本是关于Athanasius Kircher的。这表明"The last man who knew everything"这个称号也被用于其他人，因此题干的说法是正确的。


2

All Young's articles were published in Encyclopedia Britannica.

正确答案： B. FALSE

原文答案句：Between 1816 and 1825 he contributed his many and various entries to the Encyclopedia Britannica, and throughout his career, he authored numerous books, essays and papers.


对应段落：E段


审题：题目问杨的所有文章是否都发表在大英百科全书上

定位关键信息：利用"Young"、"articles"和"Encyclopedia Britannica"这些关键词定位相关信息


解题思路：原文中提到杨为大英百科全书撰写了63篇文章，但E段明确指出"throughout his career, he authored numerous books, essays and papers"，这表明杨在他的职业生涯中撰写了许多书籍、散文和论文，而不仅仅是给大英百科全书的文章。因此，题目中"All Young's articles were published in Encyclopedia Britannica"这一陈述是错误的，答案为B（False）。

3

Like others, Young wasn't so brilliant when growing up.

正确答案： B. FALSE

原文答案句：These are the landmark achievements of a man who was a child prodigy and who, unlike many remarkable children, did not disappear into oblivion as an adult.


对应段落：C段


审题：题目问Young在成长过程中是否像其他人一样不那么出色/聪明。


定位关键信息：题目中的关键词是"growing up"（成长过程中）和"wasn't so brilliant"（不那么出色/聪明）。我们需要在文章中找到关于Young童年和成长时期的信息。


解题思路：原文对应信息出现在第三段最后一句话“These are the landmark achievements of a man who was a child prodigy（天才儿童）and who，unlike many remarkable（卓越的）children, did not disappear into oblivion（遗忘）as an adul”，说明了杨在小时候就已经是一个天才儿童，长大后也没有泯然众人，所以答案是False。

4

Young's talent as a doctor surpassed his other skills.

正确答案： B. FALSE

原文答案句：Young's skill as a physician, however, did not equal his skill as a scholar of natural philosophy or linguistics.


对应段落：E段


审题：题目问的是杨作为医生的才能是否超越了他的其他技能。


定位关键信息：通过题目中的"doctor"（医生）和"skills"（技能）可以定位到E段中关于杨作为医生的技能的描述。


解题思路：原文中"Young's skill as a physician, however, did not equal his skill as a scholar of natural philosophy or linguistics."这句话表明杨作为医生的技能并不等同于他作为自然哲学或语言学学者的技能，即自然哲学或语言学学者的技能高于医生，属于原文提到考点，但方向不一致，所以答案是FALSE。

5

Young's advice was sought by people responsible for local and national issues.

正确答案： A. TRUE

原文答案句：His opinions were sought on civic and national matters, such as the introduction of gas lighting to London and methods of ship construction.


对应段落：E段


审题：题目问Young的建议是否被负责地方和国家事务的人们寻求。


定位关键信息：题干中的关键词是"advice was sought"和"local and national issues"，对应原文中的"opinions were sought"和"civic and national matters"。


解题思路：在E段中明确提到"His opinions were sought on civic and national matters"，这与题干中的"Young's advice was sought by people responsible for local and national issues"表达的意思完全一致。原文还提供了具体例子，如伦敦煤气照明和船舶建造方法的咨询，进一步证实了Young确实在地方和国家事务上被寻求建议。因此，这个陈述是正确的。


6

Young took part in various social pastimes.

正确答案： A. TRUE

原文答案句：Young was introduced into elite society, attended the theatre and learned to dance and play the flute. In addition, he was an accomplished horseman.


对应段落：F段


审题：题目问Young是否参加了各种社交娱乐活动。


定位关键信息：利用题目中的"social pastimes"（社交娱乐活动）作为关键词，在文章中寻找相关描述。


解题思路：在F段中明确提到Young被引入上流社会，参加剧院活动，学习跳舞和吹长笛，而且还是一位出色的骑手。这些都是社交娱乐活动的具体例子，因此可以确定Young确实参加了各种社交娱乐活动，所以答案为True。


7

Young suffered from a disease in his later years.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

原文答案句：无


对应段落：无


审题：题目问Thomas Young在晚年是否患有疾病。


定位关键信息：需要查找原文中是否有关于Young晚年健康状况或疾病的信息，关键词是"suffered from a disease"和"in his later years"。


解题思路：仔细阅读原文所有段落，发现文中提到了Young的生平、学术成就、职业发展和个人生活，但没有任何一处提到他在晚年患有疾病。虽然文中提到他成为医生(E段)以及他的婚姻生活(G段)，但完全没有涉及他自己的健康状况，特别是晚年是否患病。由于原文中没有提供相关信息，无法判断题目陈述的真假，因此答案应为C（NOT GIVEN）。


Questions 8-13


Answer the questions below. Choose NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER from the passage for each answer.

8

8 How many life stories did Young write for the Encyclopedia Britannica?

正确答案： 8 46
Question 8

原文答案句：Thomas Young (1773-1829) contributed 63 articles to the Encyclopedia Britannica, including 46 biographical entries (mostly on scientists and classicists) and substantial essays on 'Bridge', 'Chromatics', 'Egypt', 'Languages' and 'Tides'.


对应段落：A段


审题：题目问Young为《Encyclopedia Britannica》写了几篇人物传记(life stories)。


定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词"life stories"和"Encyclopedia Britannica"可以定位到原文A段。在原文中，"life stories"对应的是"biographical entries"，即人物传记。


解题思路：原文A段明确指出Young为《Encyclopedia Britannica》写了63篇文章，其中包括46篇人物传记(biographical entries)。题目中的"life stories"就是指"biographical entries"(人物传记)，因此答案是46。

9

9 What aspect of scientific research did Young focus on in his first academic paper?

正确答案： 9 human eye##human eye accommodation##Human Eye##Human Eye Accommodation
Question 9

原文答案句：In the paper, Young explained the process of accommodation in the human eye – on how the eye focuses properly on objects at varying distances.


对应段落：B段


审题：题目询问Thomas Young的第一篇学术论文专注于哪个方面的科学研究。


定位关键信息：通过题目中的"first academic paper"可以定位到B段中的"He presented his first paper to the Royal Society of London at the age of 20"，然后紧接着就是这篇论文的内容。


解题思路：在B段中，明确指出Young的第一篇论文解释了人眼调节的过程（accommodation in the human eye），即眼睛如何正确聚焦在不同距离的物体上。因此，答案就是"human eye accommodation"或"human eye"。


10

10 What name did Young introduce to refer to a group of languages?

正确答案： 10 Indo-European##indo-european
Question 10

原文答案句：In another entry, he coined the term Indo-European to describe the family of languages spoken throughout most of Europe and northern India.


对应段落：C段


审题：题目问Young引入了什么名称来指代一组语言。


定位关键信息：可以通过关键词"name"、"introduce"和"group of languages"来定位答案在文章中的位置。在文章中，我们需要找到Young创造或引入语言名称的相关描述。


解题思路：在C段中，明确提到"he coined the term Indo-European to describe the family of languages"，这里的"coined the term"表示创造了一个术语，与题目中的"introduce"（引入）相对应。"Indo-European"就是Young引入用来指代欧洲大部分和北印度地区语言家族的名称。因此，答案为"Indo-European"或者"indo-european"（大小写均可）。

11

11 Who inspired Young to start his medical studies?

正确答案： 11 Richard Brocklesby##richard brocklesby
Question 11

原文答案句：After leaving school, he was greatly encouraged by his mother's uncle, Richard Brocklesby, a physician and Fellow of the Royal Society. Following Brocklesby's lead, Young decided to pursue a career in medicine.


对应段落：D段


审题：题目问的是谁激励Young开始他的医学研究。


定位关键信息：题目中的关键词是"inspired"（激励）和"medical studies"（医学研究）。在文章中，我们需要找到与这些关键词相关的信息。在D段中，我们可以找到"greatly encouraged"（极大地鼓励）和"pursue a career in medicine"（从事医学职业）这些表达，它们与题目中的关键词相匹配。


解题思路：在D段中，文章明确指出Young在离开学校后，受到了他母亲的叔叔Richard Brocklesby的极大鼓励。Brocklesby是一名医生和皇家学会会员。文章接着说"Following Brocklesby's lead, Young decided to pursue a career in medicine"（跟随Brocklesby的脚步，Young决定从事医学职业）。这清楚地表明Brocklesby是激励Young开始医学研究的人。因此，答案是Richard Brocklesby。

12

12 Where did Young get a teaching position?

正确答案： 12 Royal Institution##royal institution##Royal institution
Question 12

原文答案句：Earlier, in 1801, he had been appointed to a professorship of natural philosophy at the Royal Institution, where he delivered as many as 60 lectures in a year.


对应段落：E段


审题：题目问的是Young在哪里获得了教学职位。


定位关键信息：题目中的关键词是"teaching position"（教学职位），在文章中对应的表达是"professorship"（教授职位）或"lectures"（讲座）。


解题思路：在E段中，明确提到Young在1801年被任命为皇家学会(Royal Institution)的自然哲学教授，并在那里授课。这直接回答了题目关于Young获得教学职位地点的问题。因此，答案为"Royal Institution"。

13

13 What contribution did Young make to London?

正确答案： 13 gas lighting##Gas Lighting
Question 13

原文答案句：His opinions were sought on civic and national matters, such as the introduction of gas lighting to London and methods of ship construction.


对应段落：E段


审题：题目问Young对伦敦做出了什么贡献。


定位关键信息：通过题目中的"contribution"（贡献）和"London"（伦敦）这两个关键词，可以在文章中定位到E段的相关内容。


解题思路：在E段中，明确提到"His opinions were sought on civic and national matters, such as the introduction of gas lighting to London..."，表明Young的意见被征询关于向伦敦引入煤气照明的事宜。这直接回答了题目询问Young对伦敦做出什么贡献的问题，因此答案为"gas lighting"或"Gas Lighting"。
